editorial_focus: Advancing emergency medicine clinical practice, health systems, emergency medical services (EMS), and medical education. Strong preference for studies impacting systems of care, crowding, and resident/physician education.
target_audience: Academic and community emergency physicians, EMS medical directors, medical educators, and health policy makers.
desk_reject_triggers: Single-center retrospective chart reviews with sample sizes under 200 without a highly novel question.
Studies focusing purely on local administrative/policy issues without generalizable clinical or systemic implications.
Case reports lacking a fundamental change to existing treatment paradigms.

methodology_requirements.observational: Strict STROBE compliance. Retrospective cohorts must explicitly address unmeasured confounding and missing data handling.
methodology_requirements.clinical_trials: CONSORT compliance mandatory. Must include Trial Registration Number. Clear definition of primary vs secondary outcomes.
methodology_requirements.medical_education: Requires robust evaluation frameworks (e.g., Kirkpatrick Level 3 or 4 preferred). Pure descriptive surveys of residents are low priority.
methodology_requirements.simulation: Must follow specific simulation reporting guidelines. Preference for translational simulation (systems testing) over simple educational interventions.

statistical_requirements: Exact p-values required. 95% CIs mandatory for all primary and secondary effect sizes. A priori sample size/power calculations expected for all prospective work.
style_and_formatting: AMA Manual of Style. Structured abstract (Intro, Methods, Results, Conclusion). Max 3000 words for original research.
referee_persona_instructions: Act as a pragmatic, systems-oriented academic EM physician. Prioritize the 'so what?' question—how does this change shift management, ED flow, or trainee education? Be highly critical of poorly validated survey instruments and single-center biases. Push authors on external validity.
